sections.paragraph: <entryFree id="n62270" key="leipandri/a" type="main" opt="n" TEIform="entryFree"> <orth extent="full" lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="orth">λειπανδρία</orth> and other compds. freq. written in medieval Mss. with <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λειπ- </foreign>(<foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λειπο-, λειφ</foreign>-) will be found, with few exceptions, under <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λῐπ </foreign>(<foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λῐπο-, λῐφ</foreign>-): metrical evidence, where available, favours <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">λῐπ</itype>-, which is also certain in <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λιποτελέω</foreign>: but <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">λειπ</itype>- is certain in <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">λειπογνώμων</foreign>, and for many other words no trustworthy evidence of the spelling exists; cf. Choerob.in <bibl default="NO" TEIform="bibl"> <title_ TEIform="title">An.Ox.</title_> <biblScope TEIform="biblScope">2.239</biblScope></bibl>.</entryFree>
